channel.title: Homes on Angie Lee
channel.description: Recent content in Homes on Angie Lee

item.title: About me
item.author: angiel2@illinois.edu (Angie)
item.description: Hello! I’m Angie Lee. I’m a final-year undergraduate student at the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign. My majors are {Linguistics} and {Speech and Hearing Science}. I’m currently advised by Prof. Scott Nelson. I will begin as a PhD student this Fall at the University of California San Diego’s Department of Linguistics where I will be advised by Prof. Eric Baković.

item.title: Presentations
item.author: angiel2@illinois.edu (Angie)
item.description:
• (upcoming) Lee, A. (2026). Blocking and licensing in Malay morphophonology. Talk at the International Symposium on Malay and Indonesian Linguistics 29 (ISMIL), Tampere, Finland.

  • (upcoming) Pan, Y., Cai, H., Li, A., Lee, A. (2026). Validating the elicited imitation task through eye-tracking evidence. Talk at the 47th Language Testing Research Colloquium (LTRC), Montreal, Canada.

  • Lee, A. (2026). Revisiting asymmetries in Malay morphophonology. Poster at the 62nd Annual Meeting of the Chicago Linguistic Society (CLS), Chicago, IL, USA.
